title: Pfizer — White Space
html:
Pfizer — White-Space BD Map

Prepared for: Vinoth Kumar V / Rich Durante (AO) / Nick Fleischman (AD) Last updated: 2026-04-10 Purpose: Identify, prioritize, and plan pursuit of untapped revenue across Pfizer brands x ZoomRx services

1. Brand x Service Matrix

Legend:

  • ✅ Active (contracted or in-flight)
  • 🎯 High priority white space (pursue in FY26)
  • 🔵 Medium priority (pursue FY26-27, or opportunistic)
  • ⚪ Low priority (long-term / speculative)
  • ➖ Not applicable or poor fit

Vaccines Portfolio

Service Abrysvo Maternal Abrysvo OA Prevnar Adult Prevnar Peds Trumenba/Penbraya COVID Vaccines
PET / SFEA ✅ $150K ✅ $75K 🎯 🔵 🎯 🔵

Matrix Priority Summary

Priority Count Notes
✅ Active 6 cells Abrysvo Maternal (PET, HCPP, Digital), Abrysvo OA (PET), Prevnar Adult (HCPP), Trumenba (HCPP)
🎯 High priority 14 cells Concentrated in HCPP expansion + ATU/PET upsell on existing brands
🔵 Medium priority ~60 cells Broad mid-term pipeline
⚪ Low priority ~90 cells Long-term, no current relationship or path

2. Top 10 White-Space Opportunities

Ranked by composite score of: revenue potential, ease of entry (relationship + proof points), and strategic value (does it unlock further deals).

Rank Brand Service Est. Revenue Entry Strategy Who to Pitch Timeline Rationale
1 Paxlovid HCP-Patient $75K Gavin Hansen is a warm contact via mutual referral. Pitch HCPP as a standalone first win. Use Abrysvo HCPP case study showing how real conversations surface messaging gaps that PETs miss. Gavin Hansen FY26 Q1-Q2 Directly fills the "$125K other brands" target in the AO plan. Gavin is warm. Paxlovid has complex patient decision-making (fear of COVID vs. drug concerns) that HCPP illuminates uniquely.
2 Nurtec HCP-Patient $75K No named contact yet. Ask Tim Douglas or Aimee Renaud for a warm intro to Nurtec marketing lead. Pitch HCPP as a way to understand the migraine patient journey — a space where patient voice is critical and underused. TBD (via Tim Douglas or Aimee Renaud intro) FY26 Q2 Nurtec is a major Pfizer brand in competitive migraine space. HCPP would be genuinely differentiated vs. what they likely get from Verilogue today. Fills "$125K other brands" bucket.
3 Prevnar Peds HCP-Patient $50-75K Stephanie Archer is already pitched. Follow up with Abrysvo Maternal HCPP results as proof that vaccine conversations with parents yield actionable messaging insights. Prevnar Peds and Abrysvo Maternal share the same audience (pediatricians, parents). Stephanie Archer FY26 Q1-Q2 Natural adjacency to Abrysvo Maternal — same HCP audience, same parent decision-making dynamics. Verilogue is incumbent, but HCPP differentiates on AI analysis and actionable output.
4 Abrysvo Maternal ATU $50-75K Tim Douglas already trusts ZoomRx from PET and HCPP work. Sermo is the ATU incumbent, but they deliver generic panel data. Position ZoomRx ATU as more integrated — connects to the HCPP and PET insights they already receive, creating a unified view. Tim Douglas FY26 Q2 Displaces Sermo. High strategic value: once ATU is established on one brand, it's replicable across all vaccine brands.
5 Trumenba/Penbraya Digital Tracking $50K Already in discussion with Gabby Gonzalez about tracking. Push to close. Frame as a natural extension of HCPP — "you're hearing what patients say; now see what they do online before and after." Gabby Gonzalez FY26 Q1 Almost closed. Low effort to convert. Establishes Digital Tracking as a service at Pfizer for the first time.
6 Prevnar Adult ATU $50-75K Tim Douglas / Lisa Godek just signed HCPP. Once HCPP results are delivered, present ATU as the next logical layer — "HCPP told you what happens in the exam room; ATU tells you what happens in the market." Tim Douglas / Lisa Godek FY26 Q2-Q3 Follows the playbook: HCPP first, then upsell. Creates a multi-service footprint on Prevnar Adult.
7 Trumenba/Penbraya PET $75K Gabby Gonzalez is an established contact running HCPP. Use HCPP insights to demonstrate that ZoomRx understands the meningococcal market deeply, then propose a PET that BI currently runs. Offer a head-to-head pilot at zero cost. Gabby Gonzalez FY26 Q2-Q3 Major revenue if won. But requires displacing BI, which is harder. The zero-cost pilot is the AO plan's stated Q2 strategy.
8 Prevnar Adult PET $75-100K Same approach as Trumenba. Start with HCPP delivery, then propose PET as an upgrade from BI. Tim Douglas is the decision maker for both HCPP and likely PET. Tim Douglas FY26 Q3 Follows the HCPP-to-PET upsell path. BI displacement required.
9 Xeljanz HCP-Patient $50-75K No named contact. This is a true new-team entry. Leverage Aimee Renaud (AI Excellence, above-brand) for an intro to the Xeljanz marketing lead. Pitch HCPP as an AI-forward patient insights tool. Xeljanz is in a competitive autoimmune market (vs. Humira biosimilars, Rinvoq, Skyrizi). TBD (via Aimee Renaud) FY26 Q3 First specialty care foothold outside vaccines. High strategic value even if the initial deal is small.
10 Vyndaquel HCP-Patient $50-75K Similar to Xeljanz — use Aimee Renaud or the AO plan's stated pilot-with-new-brand strategy. Vyndaquel treats transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy — a rare-ish condition where patient journey is complex and underexplored. TBD (via Aimee Renaud or Josh Stewart rare disease connection) FY26 Q3-Q4 Opens specialty care. Josh Stewart (warm contact, rare disease) may know the Vyndaquel team.

Revenue Summary of Top 10

Opportunity Low Est. High Est.
Total Top 10 $600K $775K
Realistic close rate (40-50%) $240K $390K
Needed to hit gap ($575K - $302K) $273K $273K

The top 10 opportunities, at a 40-50% close rate, cover the $273K gap. The top 5 alone at 60% close rate yield ~$195K-$225K.

3. Competitive Displacement Strategy

3.1 vs. BI (PET Incumbent)

Current state: BI runs most Pfizer PETs. They are deeply entrenched with long-running trackers and established workflows.

Displacement approach: Don't attack head-on. Flank.

  1. HCPP-to-PET pipeline. Every brand where ZoomRx wins HCPP becomes a warm lead for PET. The pitch: "Your HCPP data showed X insight about prescriber behavior. A ZoomRx PET can track whether your messaging is landing on those specific behaviors — something BI's generic tracker doesn't do because they don't have the HCPP context."
  2. Zero-cost PET pilot on one brand. Rich's AO plan already calls for this in Q2. Target: Trumenba/Penbraya or Prevnar Adult, where Gabby/Tim are established contacts. Run a one-wave pilot alongside BI's tracker. Let the data speak.
  3. AI differentiation. ZoomRx is already recognized as AI pioneers at Pfizer (Abrysvo AI work). Position PET as AI-enhanced — faster turnaround, automated insight extraction, integrated with HCPP findings. BI's PETs are traditional panel surveys with manual analysis.
  4. Don't try to replace BI everywhere at once. Win one PET on one brand. Use that as the proof point. Pfizer brands operate independently — one brand switching to ZoomRx PET doesn't threaten BI's relationship with other brands, which lowers the political risk for the client.

Realistic outcome: Win 1-2 PETs in FY26 ($75-150K), with a path to more in FY27.

3.2 vs. Verilogue (HCP-Patient Incumbent)

Current state: Verilogue does HCP-Patient conversations at Pfizer, including Prevnar Peds (Stephanie Archer's team).

Displacement approach: Differentiate on output, not methodology.

  1. AI-powered analysis. Verilogue provides transcripts and basic coding. ZoomRx HCPP provides AI-extracted themes, sentiment analysis, and actionable messaging recommendations. The deliverable is fundamentally different — it's not just "what was said" but "what it means for your brand strategy."
  2. Integration with other ZoomRx services. If a brand is doing ZoomRx HCPP + PET (or ATU), the insights connect. Verilogue's HCPP sits in isolation. This is only compelling once ZoomRx has multiple services on a brand, but it's a powerful differentiator as the relationship deepens.
  3. Coexistence is fine for now. On brands where Verilogue is entrenched and ZoomRx has no relationship (e.g., non-vaccine brands), don't lead with "replace Verilogue." Lead with "complement what you have" or offer HCPP on brands where Verilogue isn't present. Verilogue can't cover every brand.
  4. Prevnar Peds is the test case. Stephanie Archer is already pitched. If ZoomRx wins Prevnar Peds HCPP, it's a direct Verilogue displacement. Use Abrysvo Maternal HCPP results to show superior output.

Realistic outcome: Coexist on some brands, displace on 1-2 where ZoomRx has stronger relationships. Net new HCPP wins on brands Verilogue doesn't currently serve.

3.3 vs. Sermo (ATU Incumbent)

Current state: Sermo runs ATU on Abrysvo Maternal (and likely other vaccine brands).

Displacement approach: Integrated insights story.

  1. Sermo delivers ATU as a standalone product. ZoomRx can deliver ATU that connects to HCPP and PET insights on the same brand. This is the primary pitch: "One vendor, three lenses on the same market."
  2. Target Abrysvo Maternal first. ZoomRx already runs PET and HCPP there. The ATU is the missing piece. Tim Douglas sees all three deliverables — if ZoomRx ATU is even slightly better than Sermo's, the convenience of consolidation tips the scale.
  3. Pricing play. Offer a bundled price for PET + HCPP + ATU that makes it economically rational to consolidate. Sermo's standalone ATU price becomes harder to justify.
  4. Expand from there. Once ATU is proven on Abrysvo Maternal, replicate to Prevnar Adult and Trumenba.

Realistic outcome: Win Abrysvo Maternal ATU in FY26 ($50-75K). Use as proof point for other brands.

4. Connection to AO Plan — Bridging $302K to $575K

The AO plan requires $273K in net new revenue. Here's how the white-space map fills that gap.

Revenue Bridge

Source FY25 Base FY26 Target Gap to Fill White-Space Opportunity
Abrysvo Maternal $216K $225K +$9K Organic growth. Upsell ATU ($50-75K) and Message Testing ($25-50K) on top.
Abrysvo OA $0 $75K +$75K Already in plan as active. PET signed.
Prevnar Adult $0 $75K +$75K HCPP signed. Upsell to ATU ($50-75K) in H2.
Trumenba/Penbraya $86K $75K -$11K HCPP renewal. Add Digital Tracking ($50K) and pursue PET pilot ($75K).
"Other brands" $0 $125K +$125K This is where white-space is critical.
Total $302K $575K $273K

Filling the "$125K Other Brands" Bucket

This is the most uncertain part of the AO plan. Rich needs $125K from brands where ZoomRx has zero current revenue. The white-space map identifies the most viable candidates:

Priority Brand Service Est. Revenue Contact Path
1st Paxlovid HCPP $75K Gavin Hansen (warm)
2nd Nurtec HCPP $75K Via Tim Douglas or Aimee Renaud
3rd Prevnar Peds HCPP $50-75K Stephanie Archer (already pitched)
Backup Xeljanz or Vyndaquel HCPP $50-75K Via Aimee Renaud

Close 2 of these 4 and the $125K bucket is filled.

Upside Beyond $575K Target

If the core plan hits and Rich also lands:

  • Abrysvo Maternal ATU ($50-75K) — displacing Sermo
  • Trumenba Digital Tracking ($50K) — almost closed
  • One PET pilot conversion ($75K) — Trumenba or Prevnar Adult

That's $175-200K in upside, pushing toward $750K+ and setting up a FY27 target above $700K.

Quarterly Phasing (Aligned to AO Milestones)

Quarter AO Plan Milestone White-Space Actions
Q1 (Now) Use HCPP vax engagements as use cases to expand and create advocates Close Trumenba Digital Tracking. Deliver Prevnar Adult HCPP with strong results. Pitch Paxlovid HCPP to Gavin Hansen. Follow up with Stephanie Archer on Prevnar Peds.
Q2 Push to expand into PET space with zero-cost pilots Run zero-cost PET pilot on Trumenba or Prevnar Adult. Pitch Abrysvo Maternal ATU to Tim Douglas. Get intro to Nurtec team.
Q3 Convert pilots, expand Convert PET pilot to paid. Close Nurtec HCPP. Explore Xeljanz/Vyndaquel HCPP via Aimee Renaud. Close Prevnar Adult ATU.
Q4 Renewal season + upsell Renew all HCPP contracts. Bundle pricing for FY27. Pitch ATU expansion to additional brands.

5. Pfizer-Specific Entry Playbook

The Core Constraint

"Brands are structured independently, making franchise plays difficult." "Marketing leads hold significant sway."

This means:

  • No top-down selling. An above-brand champion (like Aimee Renaud) can open doors, but she cannot mandate adoption. Each brand's marketing lead decides independently.
  • No franchise deals. You cannot sell "HCPP across all vaccines" as one contract. Each brand is a separate sale, separate budget, separate decision maker.
  • Proof points must be brand-specific. Showing Abrysvo results to the Nurtec team is helpful for credibility, but the Nurtec team will judge ZoomRx on whether HCPP solves their problem, not Abrysvo's.

The Playbook: Brand-by-Brand Entry

Step 1: Identify the marketing lead. Every brand has one. They control budget and vendor decisions. If you don't know them, find them through:

  • Established contacts (Tim, Gabby, Antonietta) who may know peers on adjacent brands
  • Aimee Renaud (AI Excellence) who works across brands
  • Josh Stewart (rare disease) for specialty brands
  • Conference encounters and LinkedIn research

Step 2: Lead with HCPP. This is deliberate. HCPP is:

  • Lower cost ($50-75K) — fits within a marketing lead's discretionary budget
  • Novel — not just another survey; it's real patient-doctor conversations
  • AI-forward — Pfizer values this, and ZoomRx's AI reputation precedes it
  • Quick to deliver — results in 8-12 weeks, not a year-long tracker commitment
  • Hard to say no to — "Do you want to hear what patients actually say to their doctors about your brand?" is a compelling pitch

Step 3: Deliver exceptional results. The HCPP deliverable must be actionable, not just interesting. It must answer: "What should we change in our messaging based on what patients actually experience?" If the deliverable is a stack of transcripts with themes, you lose. If it's 3 specific messaging recommendations backed by verbatim patient quotes, you win.

Step 4: Upsell from the inside. Once HCPP is delivered and the marketing lead is satisfied:

  • ATU: "You've seen what patients say. Want to see what doctors think about your brand vs. competitors?" (Displaces Sermo.)
  • PET: "You've seen qualitative insights. Want quantitative tracking of the same themes?" (Displaces BI.)
  • Digital Tracking: "You've seen the exam room. Want to see the digital journey before and after?"
  • Message Testing: "HCPP showed messaging gaps. Want to test new messages before launch?"

Step 5: Create internal advocates. Satisfied marketing leads talk to peers. Tim Douglas's satisfaction with Abrysvo HCPP led to Prevnar Adult HCPP. Replicate this dynamic. Explicitly ask: "Is there a colleague on another brand who might benefit from this?"

Brand-Specific Entry Paths

Brand Current State Next Action Entry Path
Paxlovid Gavin Hansen is warm (referral) Schedule intro meeting. Bring Abrysvo HCPP case study. Gavin Hansen direct.
Nurtec No contact Ask Tim Douglas: "Who runs marketing insights for Nurtec?" Or ask Aimee Renaud for intro. Referral from Tim or Aimee.
Prevnar Peds Stephanie Archer already pitched Follow up with Abrysvo Maternal HCPP results as proof. Ask: "What held you back last time?" Stephanie Archer direct.
COVID Vaccines No contact Low priority — vaccine fatigue, uncertain future. Pursue only if a contact surfaces organically. Opportunistic via Tim Douglas (vaccines broadly).
Xeljanz No contact Ask Aimee Renaud for intro to Xeljanz marketing. Or Josh Stewart (rare disease may connect to immunology). Aimee Renaud or Josh Stewart referral.
Vyndaquel No contact Ask Josh Stewart (rare disease) — Vyndaquel treats a rare cardiac condition. Ask Aimee Renaud as backup. Josh Stewart or Aimee Renaud referral.
Ibrance No contact Long-term. Oncology is a separate org at Pfizer. Need an oncology-specific entry point. Use "Seagen/PFZ onco use cases" mentioned in AO plan. AO plan mentions using onco use cases — Rich may have a path here.
Xtandi / Padcev / Lorbrena No contact Same as Ibrance — oncology is a separate play. Padcev (bladder cancer) may be most interesting given recent launches. Requires oncology contact development.
Eliquis No contact Low priority. Mature brand, massive scale, likely has entrenched vendors at every level. Only if a contact surfaces.

Key People and How to Use Them

Person Role Current Relationship How to Leverage
Tim Douglas Abrysvo Maternal + Prevnar Established, repeat buyer Ask for referrals to Nurtec, COVID, other primary care brands. Upsell ATU and PET on his brands.
Gabby Gonzalez Meningococcal Established, HCPP running Close Digital Tracking. Propose PET pilot. Ask for referrals.
Antonietta Forte Abrysvo OA Established, PET running Upsell HCPP and ATU on Abrysvo OA.
Aimee Renaud AI Excellence (above-brand) Warm Key connector. Can intro ZoomRx to any brand team by framing HCPP as an AI-powered insights tool. Use her for Nurtec, Xeljanz, Vyndaquel intros.
Gavin Hansen Paxlovid Warm (mutual referral) Direct pitch target for Paxlovid HCPP.
Stephanie Archer Prevnar Peds Warm (already pitched) Re-engage with proof points from Abrysvo Maternal HCPP.
Josh Stewart Rare disease Warm Potential path to Vyndaquel and other specialty brands.
Candace Anderson Obesity Warm Pfizer's obesity pipeline is early-stage. Monitor, but not a near-term revenue play.
Renata Alves Global Vaccines Warm Global deals are harder to close and slower. Maintain relationship for long-term play.

Appendix: Service Definitions (Quick Reference)

For pitch conversations, here's what each high-priority service does in one sentence:

Service One-Liner for Pfizer Pitch
HCP-Patient (HCPP) AI analysis of real doctor-patient conversations to surface what's actually being said about your brand in the exam room.
PET / SFEA Quantitative tracker measuring physician prescribing behavior, awareness, and messaging recall over time.
ATU Awareness, trial, and usage tracking among physicians — who knows your brand, who's tried it, who keeps using it.
Digital Tracking Monitoring the digital patient journey — what patients search, read, and engage with online around your brand.
Message Testing Test new brand messages with real physicians before you launch them, using AI-powered conjoint and verbatim analysis.
OCE Omnichannel engagement measurement — is your rep + digital + medical strategy actually reaching the right doctors?
Patient Experience End-to-end patient journey mapping from diagnosis through treatment, identifying friction points and unmet needs.

This map should be refreshed quarterly or whenever a new brand/contact opens up. Next refresh: July 2026.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

3. Competitive Displacement Strategy
3.1 vs. BI (PET Incumbent)
Current state: BI runs most Pfizer PETs. They are deeply entrenched with long-running trackers and established workflows.
Displacement approach: Don't attack head-on. Flank.

HCPP-to-PET pipeline. Every brand where ZoomRx wins HCPP becomes a warm lead for PET. The pitch: "Your HCPP data showed X insight about prescriber behavior. A ZoomRx PET can track whether your messaging is landing on those specific behaviors — something BI's generic tracker doesn't do because they don't have the HCPP context."
Zero-cost PET pilot on one brand. Rich's AO plan already calls for this in Q2. Target: Trumenba/Penbraya or Prevnar Adult, where Gabby/Tim are established contacts. Run a one-wave pilot alongside BI's tracker. Let the data speak.
AI differentiation. ZoomRx is already recognized as AI pioneers at Pfizer (Abrysvo AI work). Position PET as AI-enhanced — faster turnaround, automated insight extraction, integrated with HCPP findings. BI's PETs are traditional panel surveys with manual analysis.
Don't try to replace BI everywhere at once. Win one PET on one brand. Use that as the proof point. Pfizer brands operate independently — one brand switching to ZoomRx PET doesn't threaten BI's relationship with other brands, which lowers the political risk for the client.

Realistic outcome: Win 1-2 PETs in FY26 ($75-150K), with a path to more in FY27.
3.2 vs. Verilogue (HCP-Patient Incumbent)
Current state: Verilogue does HCP-Patient conversations at Pfizer, including Prevnar Peds (Stephanie Archer's team).
Displacement approach: Differentiate on output, not methodology.

AI-powered analysis. Verilogue provides transcripts and basic coding. ZoomRx HCPP provides AI-extracted themes, sentiment analysis, and actionable messaging recommendations. The deliverable is fundamentally different — it's not just "what was said" but "what it means for your brand strategy."
Integration with other ZoomRx services. If a brand is doing ZoomRx HCPP + PET (or ATU), the insights connect. Verilogue's HCPP sits in isolation. This is only compelling once ZoomRx has multiple services on a brand, but it's a powerful differentiator as the relationship deepens.
Coexistence is fine for now. On brands where Verilogue is entrenched and ZoomRx has no relationship (e.g., non-vaccine brands), don't lead with "replace Verilogue." Lead with "complement what you have" or offer HCPP on brands where Verilogue isn't present. Verilogue can't cover every brand.
Prevnar Peds is the test case. Stephanie Archer is already pitched. If ZoomRx wins Prevnar Peds HCPP, it's a direct Verilogue displacement. Use Abrysvo Maternal HCPP results to show superior output.

Realistic outcome: Coexist on some brands, displace on 1-2 where ZoomRx has stronger relationships. Net new HCPP wins on brands Verilogue doesn't currently serve.
3.3 vs. Sermo (ATU Incumbent)
Current state: Sermo runs ATU on Abrysvo Maternal (and likely other vaccine brands).
Displacement approach: Integrated insights story.

Sermo delivers ATU as a standalone product. ZoomRx can deliver ATU that connects to HCPP and PET insights on the same brand. This is the primary pitch: "One vendor, three lenses on the same market."
Target Abrysvo Maternal first. ZoomRx already runs PET and HCPP there. The ATU is the missing piece. Tim Douglas sees all three deliverables — if ZoomRx ATU is even slightly better than Sermo's, the convenience of consolidation tips the scale.
Pricing play. Offer a bundled price for PET + HCPP + ATU that makes it economically rational to consolidate. Sermo's standalone ATU price becomes harder to justify.
Expand from there. Once ATU is proven on Abrysvo Maternal, replicate to Prevnar Adult and Trumenba.

Realistic outcome: Win Abrysvo Maternal ATU in FY26 ($50-75K). Use as proof point for other brands.

5. Pfizer-Specific Entry Playbook
The Core Constraint

"Brands are structured independently, making franchise plays difficult."
"Marketing leads hold significant sway."

This means:

No top-down selling. An above-brand champion (like Aimee Renaud) can open doors, but she cannot mandate adoption. Each brand's marketing lead decides independently.
No franchise deals. You cannot sell "HCPP across all vaccines" as one contract. Each brand is a separate sale, separate budget, separate decision maker.
Proof points must be brand-specific. Showing Abrysvo results to the Nurtec team is helpful for credibility, but the Nurtec team will judge ZoomRx on whether HCPP solves their problem, not Abrysvo's.

The Playbook: Brand-by-Brand Entry
Step 1: Identify the marketing lead. Every brand has one. They control budget and vendor decisions. If you don't know them, find them through:

Established contacts (Tim, Gabby, Antonietta) who may know peers on adjacent brands
Aimee Renaud (AI Excellence) who works across brands
Josh Stewart (rare disease) for specialty brands
Conference encounters and LinkedIn research

Step 2: Lead with HCPP. This is deliberate. HCPP is:

Lower cost ($50-75K) — fits within a marketing lead's discretionary budget
Novel — not just another survey; it's real patient-doctor conversations
AI-forward — Pfizer values this, and ZoomRx's AI reputation precedes it
Quick to deliver — results in 8-12 weeks, not a year-long tracker commitment
Hard to say no to — "Do you want to hear what patients actually say to their doctors about your brand?" is a compelling pitch

Step 3: Deliver exceptional results. The HCPP deliverable must be actionable, not just interesting. It must answer: "What should we change in our messaging based on what patients actually experience?" If the deliverable is a stack of transcripts with themes, you lose. If it's 3 specific messaging recommendations backed by verbatim patient quotes, you win.
Step 4: Upsell from the inside. Once HCPP is delivered and the marketing lead is satisfied:

ATU: "You've seen what patients say. Want to see what doctors think about your brand vs. competitors?" (Displaces Sermo.)
PET: "You've seen qualitative insights. Want quantitative tracking of the same themes?" (Displaces BI.)
Digital Tracking: "You've seen the exam room. Want to see the digital journey before and after?"
Message Testing: "HCPP showed messaging gaps. Want to test new messages before launch?"

Step 5: Create internal advocates. Satisfied marketing leads talk to peers. Tim Douglas's satisfaction with Abrysvo HCPP led to Prevnar Adult HCPP. Replicate this dynamic. Explicitly ask: "Is there a colleague on another brand who might benefit from this?"
